gene_region.label: SOX10 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical SOX10 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: SOX10 encodes a neural-crest transcription factor required for melanocyte and glial lineage development. This topic bundle emphasizes hair pigmentation and graying, with interpretation centered on melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local SOX10 bundle is oriented toward hair pigmentation and graying. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: SOX10 variants can alter, tag, or contextualize melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Hair pigmentation, skin pigmentation, and graying research context
Melanocyte development, survival, and melanosome biology
Pigmentary trait, albinism, melanoma-lineage, and follicle biology
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: SOX10 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for SOX10.
Because SOX10 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use SOX10 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology.
Hair pigmentation, skin pigmentation, and graying research context
Melanocyte development, survival, and melanosome biology

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000100146: SOX10 coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: SOX10 biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: SOX10: hair pigmentation and graying via melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a SOX10 hair pigmentation and graying thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_records.common_name: SOX10 hair pigmentation and graying pathway model
variant_records.clinical_interpretation: SOX10 pigmentation variation model is curated as a SOX10 marker for Hair pigmentation, skin pigmentation, and graying research context, Melanocyte development, survival, and melanosome biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag melanocyte development, melanin synthesis and trafficking, hair-follicle pigmentation, melanocyte stem-cell maintenance, and graying biology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Hair pigmentation, skin pigmentation, and graying research context
Melanocyte development, survival, and melanosome biology
Pigmentary trait, albinism, melanoma-lineage, and follicle biology
research_context: Use this marker as a SOX10 pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.usual_variant_note: SOX10 hair pigmentation and graying pathway model
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair SOX10 pigmentation variation model with SOX10 methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
